message:
fix(web): implement case-insensitive HTTP headers throughout web stack and fix Unix datagram unnamed addr

HTTP headers are case-insensitive per RFC 9110 but the web stack was storing
and matching them as-is, causing missed header lookups when casing differed
between client and server. Fix comprehensively:
- Request::with_header() now normalizes names to lowercase on insertion
- New Request::header() method does case-insensitive lookup (fast path for
  already-normalized, fallback to eq_ignore_ascii_case scan)
- Response::header() normalizes to lowercase
- StaticFiles::header() normalizes custom headers
- IntoResponse for (StatusCode, Vec<(String,String)>, T) normalizes
- RequestContext::header() delegates to Request::header()
- WebSocket upgrade validation simplified: removes duplicate .get("upgrade")
  .or_else(|| .get("Upgrade")) chains in favor of single .header() calls
- RequestIdMiddleware and RequestTraceMiddleware normalize header names at
  construction via normalize_header_name()
- StaticFilesHandler uses req.header() for If-None-Match ETag matching

TcpListener::accept() simplified to use poll_fn directly instead of a
redundant closure-wrapping async block. The conformance TcpListener trait
updated to take &mut self for accept() to match the implementation.

UnixDatagram::socket_addr_from_unix_addr() now handles unnamed sockets
correctly: checks if the address length is <= sun_path offset (indicating an
unnamed socket) and synthesizes a proper unnamed SocketAddr via a temporary
unbound datagram, instead of the previous incorrect SocketAddr::from_pathname("")
which could fail on some platforms.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
